./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:15:{"id":"corpus-0e60d121f56c","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded","description":"Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).\n\n## Two-part bug\n\n### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)\n\nInitial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.\n\nWe assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No route-targets loaded' error instead of 'unknown route-target'.\n\nVerified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, route-target loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.\n\n### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice\n\nThe routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare route-target names against an LM Studio upstream-service. The standard discovery flow should be:\n\n1. GET /v1/route-targets → enumerate the catalog the server is offering\n2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)\n3. Cache the (upstream-service, route-target_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL\n4. When user code asks for 'route-target qwen3-coder-next' against this upstream-service, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list\n\nThis converts the 'No route-targets loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested route-target qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).\n\n## Impact on cost-tiered routing\n\nThe cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable upstream-service readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:\n- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation\n- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input\n- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier\n\n## Fix scope\n\n- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID\n- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)\n- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)\n- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)","acceptance":"(1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. (2) Bare route-target names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) upstream-services command shows http-connected and inference-ready-route-targets per upstream-service. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-route-targets, not 'connected (N route-targets)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.","labels":["kind:bug","area:routing","area:discovery","upstream-service:lmstudio"],"bead":{"id":"agent-40ee5960","title":"bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).\n\n## Two-part bug\n\n### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)\n\nInitial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.\n\nWe assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No route-targets loaded' error instead of 'unknown route-target'.\n\nVerified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, route-target loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.\n\n### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice\n\nThe routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare route-target names against an LM Studio upstream-service. The standard discovery flow should be:\n\n1. GET /v1/route-targets → enumerate the catalog the server is offering\n2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)\n3. Cache the (upstream-service, route-target_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL\n4. When user code asks for 'route-target qwen3-coder-next' against this upstream-service, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list\n\nThis converts the 'No route-targets loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested route-target qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).\n\n## Impact on cost-tiered routing\n\nThe cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable upstream-service readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:\n- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation\n- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input\n- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier\n\n## Fix scope\n\n- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID\n- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)\n- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)\n- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)","acceptance":"(1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. (2) Bare route-target names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) upstream-services command shows http-connected and inference-ready-route-targets per upstream-service. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-route-targets, not 'connected (N route-targets)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.","labels":["kind:bug","area:routing","area:discovery","upstream-service:lmstudio"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:21:{"id":"corpus-148d699c1390","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one upstream-service-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.\nSuggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed upstream-service-reported/configured sessions.","acceptance":"CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed upstream-service-reported/configured session example.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-7853958b","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:22:{"id":"corpus-1565d82e7505","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"cache: cost attribution at the native loop using already-existing manifest fields","description":"Bead D of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Wire the cache-token costs that Anthropic and OpenAI already report into core.CostAttribution. Today the configured-cost fallback at internal/core/loop.go:303 prices only Input + Output; cache-read tokens are counted at full input price for cost attribution even though Anthropic/OpenAI charge ~10% / ~50% of base. Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy=\"off\" semantics.\n\nCodex correctly diagnosed the wiring: manifest v4 already has CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM at internal/route-targetcatalog/manifest.go:44-45 (route-target entries) and :139-140 (target entries) with validation. CostAttribution already has CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount at internal/core/agent.go:198-199. Telemetry emission already wires them at internal/core/loop.go:936-940. The only missing piece is: PricingFor at internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 drops the cache fields when projecting to core.Route-targetPricing, and core.Route-targetPricing at internal/core/pricing.go:5 lacks the slots, so loop.go:303 has no source-of-truth to read.\n\nThree coordinated edits:\n\n1. internal/core/pricing.go:5 — add CacheReadPerM, CacheWritePerM float64 to core.Route-targetPricing.\n\n2. internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 (PricingFor) — preserve CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM from the manifest route-target/target onto the returned Route-targetPricing.\n\n3. internal/core/loop.go:303 — extend the configured-cost computation to add cache-read and cache-write costs:\n   computed := input/1e6 * InputPerMTok + output/1e6 * OutputPerMTok\n            + cacheRead/1e6 * CacheReadPerM\n            + cacheWrite/1e6 * CacheWritePerM\n   Source the cache token counts from resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens and CacheWriteTokens. Populate CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = \u0026cacheReadCost and CacheWriteAmount = \u0026cacheWriteCost (slots already there).\n\n4. Honor CachePolicy=\"off\" from bead A: when off, emit CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. nil = runner/upstream-service didn't report; explicit zero = caller asked for no caching. This matches the existing zero-vs-unknown convention from agent-a8cbdb87.\n\n5. Update CONTRACT-003 prose: paragraph in the Cost Attribution / UsageReport section explaining that cache_read and cache_write tokens are priced at the manifest's CostCacheReadPerM/CostCacheWritePerM rates; nil cache amounts mean the runner or upstream-service didn't report cache usage; explicit zero means caching was opted out via CachePolicy=\"off\".\n\nHistorical session-log recalculation is OUT OF SCOPE per codex: existing logs cannot be re-priced reliably from UsageReport alone without changing semantics. New runs price correctly going forward; old logs keep their existing numbers.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- internal/core/pricing.go (struct fields)\n- internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go (PricingFor preservation)\n- internal/core/loop.go (cost computation at line 303)\n- internal/core/loop_test.go (or new file: cache-cost test cases)\n- internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog_test.go (PricingFor preservation test)\n- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md (cost-semantics prose)\n\nOut of scope:\n- Anthropic cache_control writes (bead C)\n- OpenAI prefix-stability test (bead B)\n- UsageReport historical recalculation\n- New CHANGELOG entry beyond what the bead's commit message produces","acceptance":"1. core.Route-targetPricing has CacheReadPerM and CacheWritePerM float64 fields. Native loop populates cache cost: TestNativeLoopPopulatesCacheCostAttribution drives a fixture catalog through a virtual upstream-service that emits resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens=1000 and CacheWriteTokens=500. Asserts CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = (1000 / 1e6) * CostCacheReadPerM and CacheWriteAmount = (500 / 1e6) * CostCacheWritePerM, both as non-nil *float64.\n4. CachePolicy=off semantics: TestCachePolicyOffZerosCacheAttribution drives the same loop with ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy=\"off\"; asserts CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount are non-nil *float64 with value 0.0 (NOT nil).\n5. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:33:{"id":"corpus-2205629c24fa","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-264e7ea0","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:42:{"id":"corpus-2b7a289b6389","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"routing: effort/permissions gating per upstream-service+route-target (not just per runner)","description":"## Upstream-service-specific honoring of --effort and --permissions (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).\n\nToday, Runner.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so runneres without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But upstream-service-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning route-targets, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every runner+upstream-service combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).\n  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (upstream-service, route-target).\n  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.\n- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (upstream-service, route-target) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.\n- Document expected behavior for each known upstream-service in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning route-targets = no effort, etc.).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).\n- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.\n\n## Files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go\n- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go","acceptance":"1. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:43:{"id":"corpus-2cc173f3fffd","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"...\"}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}' -upstream-service nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -\u003e `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == \"prompt\"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"}` still fails validation.","acceptance":"A plain JSON prompt like {\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-5e455139","title":"correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:56:{"id":"corpus-399a45730bb9","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification","description":"## Symptom\n\nSessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:\n\n```\nagent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {\"message\":\"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI\"}\n```\n\nObserved against `surface=embedded-openai`. The agent surfaces this as a generic \"upstream-service error\" and aborts the session.\n\n## Why it matters\n\nThis is a **server-capability** error — the local OpenAI-compat server (probably mlx_lm / lmstudio variant) cannot handle a quantized rotating KV cache for the route-target+config combination. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different route-target). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\nIn `upstream-service/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):\n\n1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the upstream-service) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.\n2. Emit a clear human message: \"Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this route-target. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a route-target/config it supports.\"\n3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.\n4. Optional: add a routing hint so this upstream-service+route-target combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.\n\n## Related\n\nCounterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related \"reasoning stall\" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).","acceptance":"1. When a streaming response contains NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI (or any NotImplementedError from the upstream-service), the agent emits a structured error with a stable code (e.g. UPSTREAM-SERVICE_CAPABILITY_MISSING) and a human-readable message naming the missing capability.\n2. The error is non-retryable in the routing layer — agent does not silently retry on the same upstream-service+route-target.\n3. A unit test fixture replays a recorded SSE stream containing this error and asserts the structured classification.\n4. Behavior verified against ddx replay of the failing session in sessions.jsonl.","labels":["kind:bug","area:upstream-service"],"bead":{"id":"agent-a8915e01","title":"BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Symptom\n\nSessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:\n\n```\nagent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {\"message\":\"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI\"}\n```\n\nObserved against `surface=embedded-openai`. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:57:{"id":"corpus-3a8ca320faaa","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: document Playwright build prerequisites","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:147\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The updated ADR now shows the embedded Playwright config launching `${cliRoot}/build/ddx server --port 18080`, but the run instructions still say only `bun run test:e2e`. That command does not build `cli/build/ddx` or refresh the embedded frontend bundle. On a clean checkout, or after frontend changes, following the ADR literally can fail because the binary is missing or stale. CI avoids this by running `bun run build` in `cli/internal/server/frontend` and `make build` in `cli` before `bun run test:e2e`.\nSuggested fix: Update ADR-002's Playwright run instructions to state the required build prerequisites explicitly, or change the documented workflow to invoke a command that builds the frontend bundle and CLI binary before running Playwright.","acceptance":"ADR-002 documents the required preconditions for `bun run test:e2e` so the steps mention building the frontend bundle and `cli/build/ddx` first, consistent with `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and CI.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-8f988a16","title":"correctness: document Playwright build prerequisites","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:147\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The updated ADR now shows the embedded Playwright config launching `${cliRoot}/build/ddx server --port 18080`, but the run instructions still say only `bun run test:e2e`. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:63:{"id":"corpus-42801b4c2e9b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN upstream-service error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking route-targets hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning route-targets can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the runner considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the runner is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud upstream-services (claude, codex) and local upstream-services (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking route-targets routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per upstream-service, per route-target, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking route-target the timeout doesn't mean 'broken upstream-service', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","acceptance":"AC1. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[upstream-service].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per upstream-service.\n\nAC2. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-tier via the route-target-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking route-target (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat route-targets keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When Upstream-serviceTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-route-target surfaces (upstream-service.type=lmstudio AND route-target name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:runner","kind:design-flaw"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-2c63bb95","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud upstream-services (claude, codex) and local upstream-services (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking route-targets routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per upstream-service, per route-target, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking route-target the timeout doesn't mean 'broken upstream-service', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","acceptance":"AC1. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[upstream-service].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per upstream-service.\n\nAC2. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-tier via the route-target-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking route-target (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat route-targets keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When Upstream-serviceTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-route-target surfaces (upstream-service.type=lmstudio AND route-target name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:runner","kind:design-flaw"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:83:{"id":"corpus-564806177b8d","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve doctor audit for legacy untyped plugin installs","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/doctor.go:454\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:87:{"id":"corpus-5a62fb11294c","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"runner-test: decide PTY cassette transport for live TUI record/replay","description":"Make the transport decision for golden-master testing of interactive runneres before building cassette-heavy integration tests. The prior discussion rejected a small incidental tmux dependency: either the project standardizes on tmux for the whole runner lifecycle, or it owns PTY lifecycle directly. If the reusable cassette player is generic enough to be its own project, define that boundary instead of burying it inside agent.\n\nThe decision must evaluate:\n- direct PTY ownership using Go primitives and os/exec\n- tmux as the session/process lifecycle manager\n- existing tools such as ntm or other terminal/TUI snapshot approaches if locally relevant\n- how to record input/output bytes, screen snapshots, environment, resize events, exit status, timing, and structured service events\n- how replay is deterministic without credentials\n- how record mode fails fast when the target runner lacks credentials/auth/subscription/quota\n- how users can inspect an in-flight or recorded run without corrupting the cassette\n\nThis bead does not implement the cassette runner. It creates the architecture decision and follow-up constraints for implementation beads.","acceptance":"1. A design or ADR document under docs/helix/02-design records the chosen PTY/cassette transport and explicitly rejects the alternatives with reasons.\n2. The decision states whether tmux is a required dependency for runner execution, not a partial helper, or whether agent owns PTY lifecycle directly.\n3. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:93:{"id":"corpus-612aeaa9052a","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.\n\nContext:\n- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats route-target only reports route-target/session usage after an API call. It did not show quota remaining/limit/reset for the active concrete route-target.\n- The same PTY session showed /route-target manage is the quota surface. Observed output included: Flash 4% used, reset 9:13 PM (23h 46m); Flash Lite 0% used, reset 9:27 PM (24h); Pro 100% used.\n- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats route-target shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the route-target management dialog. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.\n- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.\n- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.\n- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.\n- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.\n- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.\n- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.","acceptance":"1. Add a Gemini PTY quota probe or cassette-backed parser that can parse /route-target manage output containing tier/route-target usage percentages and reset times, including exhausted tiers such as Pro 100% used.\n2. Persist parsed Gemini quota evidence in a durable cache with source, captured_at, freshness, per-tier/route-target used percent or remaining/limit, and reset time.\n3. ListRunneres reports Gemini quota only from parsed quota evidence, never from auth/account freshness alone. Missing or stale Gemini quota evidence is not ok.\n4. Automatic routing does not select Gemini when quota evidence is missing/stale or the selected tier/route-target is exhausted; if promotion is enabled, it is gated by fresh parsed quota evidence.\n5. Add tests covering the observed PTY output: Flash 4% used with reset, Flash Lite 0% used with reset, and Pro 100% used/exhausted.\n6. go test ./... passes.","labels":["area:runner","area:gemini","kind:feature"],"bead":{"id":"agent-37659612","title":"Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.\n\nContext:\n- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats route-target only reports route-target/session usage after an API call. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.\n- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.\n- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.\n- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.\n- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.\n- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.\n- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.","acceptance":"1. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:115:{"id":"corpus-7a93d490ad8e","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --runner agent --route-target qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-25a41f51","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:117:{"id":"corpus-7c1aedcf73aa","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable","description":"Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. This is wrong on multiple axes:\n\n1. Push race is fundamentally recoverable: pull/merge/push is the standard fix and takes seconds.\n2. A 1-year cooldown means the bead never re-runs in any reasonable working horizon.\n3. The work is already done locally — the bead is functionally complete; reopening it for a year is misleading.\n\nObserved in this session on ddx-98e6e9ef (routing-preflight-gate): worker landed commit 978131c6, push rejected because origin/main had advanced (687fb4ca + 4a6c6e8a pushed remotely between the local land and the push). Loop set retry_after=2027-04-29T03:43:59Z. Operator had to manually merge, push, and clear the cooldown.\n\nWhat is needed:\n\n1. push_failed should auto-pull/merge/retry-push at least once before giving up. The merge case where origin advanced with non-overlapping work is the common case in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup.\n\n2. If the auto-merge has conflicts that the loop cannot resolve, push_failed should set a SHORT cooldown (5-15 min), not 1 year. The operator just needs time to resolve the conflict.\n\n3. push_failed-with-conflicts should mark the bead as needing-human-review (a structured outcome similar to declined_needs_decomposition from ddx-fba752b9), not as a generic execution failure.\n\n4. The loop's cooldown duration should NEVER exceed some reasonable cap (e.g. 24h) for any outcome. Year-scale cooldowns mean 'never' and that should be a deliberate operator choice via 'ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...', not an automatic loop decision.","acceptance":"1. push_failed flow attempts auto pull --rebase / merge / retry-push at least once before exiting with push_failed. 2. push_failed cooldown caps at 24h regardless of underlying error. 3. push_failed-with-conflicts (auto-merge fails) emits a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed; bead is parked for human review with the conflict context recorded as a structured event (kind:push-conflict). 4. Regression test: a fake git that returns non-fast-forward on first push, then fast-forward after pull/merge, exercises the new flow and asserts the bead lands cleanly without operator intervention. 5. Regression test: a fake git that produces an unresolvable merge conflict exercises the structured-outcome path and asserts cooldown \u003c= 24h. 6. CHANGELOG entry. 7. Update ddx-fba752b9's CHANGELOG section (or a new section) noting that the 24h cap applies to all loop-set cooldowns, not just push failures.","labels":["execute-loop","beads","quality-of-life"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-a458af7c","title":"execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:125:{"id":"corpus-84b3a485c4f4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.","acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:agent","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-a51dd27a","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:126:{"id":"corpus-862310dae8d9","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:518\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:130:{"id":"corpus-8da9c3bacad5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:80\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.\nSuggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:131:{"id":"corpus-8e69723cf1b4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:649\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.\nSuggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-939516c7","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:649\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.\nSuggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:139:{"id":"corpus-9725c274b8e3","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"bug: compaction loop can enter stuck state — no_compaction=true repeating without bound","description":"The compaction package can enter a stuck state where compaction.end events fire with no_compaction=true and success=false repeatedly, pulsing the inactivity timer on every iteration so the caller's timeout never fires. Downstream consumer DDx ran into this mid-session on a production worker: ~8461 stuck-compaction events accumulated before the hosting process had to be killed.\n\n## Evidence\n\nDDx-side session log:\n/Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\nParent process killed at PID 4141811 after wedging for hours on a completed task.\n\n## DDx-side workaround (defense in depth, does not fix the root cause)\n\nCommit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 in ddx repo (bead ddx-25a41f51) wraps the embedded agent call with two guards:\n1. compaction.start and compaction.end(no_compaction=true) events no longer pulse the caller's inactivity timer — only real-progress events (LLM calls, tool results, commits) extend the timeout.\n2. Circuit breaker counts consecutive compaction.end(success=false, no_compaction=true) events and aborts with a structured compaction_stuck error after a configurable threshold (default 50).\n\nThe workaround catches the stuck state at the DDx/caller layer. The native fix belongs in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent so every ddx-agent user (not just DDx CLI) benefits.\n\n## Requested upstream fix\n\n- Compaction driver detects its own stuck state via the same heuristic (consecutive success=false/no_compaction=true events) and returns a structured error to the caller\n- Eliminates the need for the caller to interpret compaction events as activity signals\n- Exposes a clean API signal so consumers can decide whether to retry, escalate, or abort\n- Regression test seeds the stuck state and verifies the driver bails instead of looping\n\n## Scope\n\n- Bounded attempt counter inside the compaction loop (not just the caller)\n- Structured error: compaction.ErrStuck or similar\n- Integration test using a deterministic seed that reproduces the 33MB jsonl pattern\n\n## Relationship to DDx\n\nWhen this lands upstream, DDx can remove its circuit-breaker workaround and rely on the native error. Until then, DDx keeps both layers as belt-and-suspenders.","acceptance":"(1) Compaction loop bounded with a structural stop. (2) Native structured error signals stuck state to callers. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:144:{"id":"corpus-9d9f71c781c1","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: prefix-aware compaction still overflows when summary exceeds remaining budget","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: compaction/summarize.go:114; compaction/summarize.go:326; compaction/summarize.go:363; compaction/summarize.go:377\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The last-commit fix retries compaction with later cut points until only the active user prompt is preserved, but it never budgets the generated summary itself. A direct repro in the current tree with ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, a 120-character system prompt prefix (prefixTokens=32), five 120-character history turns, and a mock summarizer that returns 100 characters produces TokensAfter=89 with the final compacted request reduced to only DO-THE-THING plus the injected summary. That still exceeds the 80-token budget the commit claims to enforce.\nSuggested fix: budget the summary against the remaining window after prefix tokens, preserved messages, and wrapper overhead are accounted for; trim or bound the injected summary when needed, and do not return a successful compaction result whose final request still exceeds the effective window. A direct repro in the current tree with ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, a 120-character system prompt prefix (prefixTokens=32), five 120-character history turns, and a mock summarizer that returns 100 characters produces TokensAfter=89 with the final compacted request reduced to only DO-THE-THING plus the injected summary. That still exceeds the 80-token budget the commit claims to enforce.\nSuggested fix: budget the summary against the remaining window after prefix tokens, preserved messages, and wrapper overhead are accounted for; trim or bound the injected summary when needed, and do not return a successful compaction result whose final request still exceeds the effective window. Add a regression test that uses the 120-character prefix / 100-character summary repro and asserts the final compacted request stays within budget while preserving the active prompt.","acceptance":"With ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, a 120-character system-prefix token estimate, and a mock summarizer returning 100 characters, NewCompactor preserves the active user prompt and returns a compacted request whose TokensAfter is \u003c= 80; add regression coverage for this exact overflow case.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:150:{"id":"corpus-a4c8352b5628","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. When the system prompt is larger than the keep budget, FindCutPoint now returns len(messages), so CompactMessages summarizes the entire live history, including the current user prompt. A repro with ContextWindow=25, KeepRecentTokens=5, and an 80-character system prompt shows the final upstream-service call receives only the system prompt plus the generated summary; the explicit user prompt  is no longer present as a real user message. SD-006 says session prefix messages are excluded from preserved user messages and shows post-compaction ordering that retains recent user messages after the system prompt. Compacting away the active prompt lets the summarizer paraphrase or omit the instruction that the next route-target turn is supposed to answer.\nSuggested fix: Account for system-prompt tokens in the compaction trigger and post-compaction fit calculation without letting them reduce the preserved recent-conversation budget to zero. Preserve at least the last real user message/current prompt verbatim, or add cut-point logic that excludes prefix context from the keep budget while still enforcing a bounded final request size.","acceptance":"With a large system prompt and a small KeepRecentTokens budget, compaction still triggers when required but the next upstream-service call retains the active user prompt as a verbatim user message; add a regression test that fails if the final upstream-service request contains only system prompt plus summary after compaction.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-674e2d05","title":"correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. Preserve at least the last real user message/current prompt verbatim, or add cut-point logic that excludes prefix context from the keep budget while still enforcing a bounded final request size.","acceptance":"With a large system prompt and a small KeepRecentTokens budget, compaction still triggers when required but the next upstream-service call retains the active user prompt as a verbatim user message; add a regression test that fails if the final upstream-service request contains only system prompt plus summary after compaction.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

The methods themselves are likely just thin wrappers around Runner.Config + free helpers — extract the helpers, drop the methods.\n- For tmux quota refresh: call agent module helpers directly (internal/runneres/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go via the existing tmux probe ENTRY POINTS; the agent has these functions exported, e.g. ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux from internal/runneres/claude — check)\n\nAfter this lands:\n- Runner public methods all gone\n- Runner struct can delete (Phase 5g — separate bead OR fold here)\n- The 5 file-delete beads (ddx-f0cab7b3 state+registry, ddx-ea7f041c routing.go, ddx-70c48576 routing observability, ddx-1bf6c0df tier_escalation, ddx-2912e7cd discovery+deadline+probe) cascade cleanly","acceptance":"- [ ] observability_workdir.go does not build a Runner internally\n- [ ] Runner.ProbeRunnerState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux methods deleted\n- [ ] Runner struct fields (Registry, Discovery, Catalog, AgentUpstream-service, etc.) — only those needed by REMAINING test scaffolding kept; rest deleted\n- [ ] Build clean; existing tests pass\n- [ ] After this lands, the 5 file-delete beads execute cleanly","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","kind:cleanup"],"parent":"ddx-ac5c7fdb"}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:166:{"id":"corpus-b7113543d6ca","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Data route-target\n\nAdd to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:\n\n- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.\n- `CostSource string` — `\"catalog\" | \"subscription\" | \"unknown\" | \"user-config\"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.\n\nPopulate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:\n\n- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:\n  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).\n  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).\n  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).\n  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.\n  - `CostSource: \"subscription\"`.\n- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: \"unknown\"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.\n\n### Scoring changes\n\n`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:\n1. Eligible\n2. Quota OK / healthy\n3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged\n4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new\n5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)\n6. Name\n\nFor unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: \"unknown\"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.\n\n### Unknown-cost policy\n\nA documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: \"unknown\"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.\n\n## Subscription-quota pricing curve\n\nDefault curve (tunable):\n- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0\n- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target\n- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token\n- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)","acceptance":"1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.\n2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.\n3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.\n4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).\n5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.\n6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.\n8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.\n9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).\n10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.","labels":["routing","cost","routing-plan"],"bead":{"id":"agent-0dafc7f0","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:172:{"id":"corpus-bc463032ef8f","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Tighten cmd/agent boundary enforcement and CI checks","description":"The existing boundary tests only ban agentcore.Run in cmd/agent/main.go and require DecodeServiceEvent on the execute path. Replace the permissive allowlist with the minimal set that truly has no public replacement, add negative checks for forbidden internal packages and symbols, and wire the check into the normal test path/CI gate used for cmd/agent. If any CLI-visible behavior still needs a forbidden internal package, that is a blocker to fix in code or by extending the public contract first.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cmd/agent/service_boundary_test.go\n- any helper test files or CI/test scripts needed to enforce the boundary\n- contributor guidance if it needs to point at the executable boundary spec\n\nOut of scope:\n- The actual routing/session-log/upstream-service refactors except as needed to make the stricter boundary tests pass","acceptance":"1. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:180:{"id":"corpus-c3f5ee3f3c3c","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add project-scoped MCP tools: ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware tool selection","description":"## Scope\nAdd project-awareness to the MCP tool layer per SD-019 requirements.\n\n## Context\nFEAT-002/SD-019 requires ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware selection semantics for all existing project-local MCP tools. Currently, all 18 MCP tools operate against the single working directory without project scoping.\n\n## Implementation approach\n1. Add ddx_list_projects tool — returns project registry entries\n2. Add ddx_show_project tool — returns single project detail by ID\n3. Add optional \"project\" parameter to all project-local tools\n4. When project param is provided, resolve via GetProjectByID and use that project's path\n5. When omitted and exactly one project registered, auto-resolve (singleton compat)\n6. When omitted and \u003e1 project registered, return error requesting disambiguation\n7.
