fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01JHtqnETNPSPjL7zTuc9Tnt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Rv6VWw33HurET6YfGKMM4k","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs","content":"//! JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\n//!\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::extract::State;\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    inner: RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>,\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    /// Create an empty cache.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\n    pub issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc<InMemoryRevocationCache>,\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\n    pub storage: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Convenience constructor.\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// Router::new()\n///     .route(\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\", …)\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n///         Arc::new(state),\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\n///     ))\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\n\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\n    }\n\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n    })\n}\n\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Extract the `Bearer <token>` value from the `Authorization` header.\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n        .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":283,"cache_read_input_tokens":118515,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":283},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"6e2eb73d-c717-4746-9980-0fe22b34a94b"}

issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    inner: RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>,\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    /// Create an empty cache.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\n    pub issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc<InMemoryRevocationCache>,\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\n    pub storage: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Convenience constructor.\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// Router::new()\n///     .route(\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\", …)\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n///         Arc::new(state),\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\n///     ))\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\n\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\n    }\n\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n    })\n}\n\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Extract the `Bearer <token>` value from the `Authorization` header.\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n        .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build state with a suspended user.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    // Overwrite the user with a suspended version.\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Suspended\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: Some(1000),\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"user_suspended\");\n}\n\n/// Row 10: `sub` not a tenant member → 403 not_a_tenant_member\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_tenant_member() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Storage with user but NO tenant membership.\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"No Member\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"not_a_tenant_member\");\n}\n\n/// Row 11: URL database not in grants → 403 database_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_database_not_granted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant is for \"products\", URL database is \"orders\"\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"products\".to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"database_not_granted\");\n}\n\n/// Row 12: Op not in grant → 403 op_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_op_not_granted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant only has Read; we'll POST (Write).\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n\n    // POST = Write, but grant only has Read.\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"POST\")\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"op_not_granted\");\n}\n\n// ── valid_jwt_populates_request_extension ─────────────────────────────────────\n\nasync fn identity_handler(\n    Extension(identity): Extension<ResolvedIdentity>,\n) -> impl IntoResponse {\n    axum::Json(serde_json::json!({\"user_id\": identity.user_id.as_str()}))\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn valid_jwt_populates_request_extension() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(identity_handler))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ));\n\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n    assert_eq!(json[\"user_id\"].as_str().unwrap(), USER_ID);\n}\n\n// ── revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second ─────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app_before = make_app(state.clone());\n    let app_after = make_app(state.clone());\n\n    // First request must succeed.\n    let (status, _) = send(app_before, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK, \"before revocation should be 200\");\n\n    // Directly invalidate the cache — no sleep needed.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    // Second request must fail with credential_revoked.\n    let (status, json) = send(app_after, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n// ── clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected ─────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_exp_29s_accepted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 29; // 29 s ago — within the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, _) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_exp_31s_rejected() {\n    let issuer = token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, json) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_nbf_29s_future_accepted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 29; // 29 s from now — within the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, _) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_nbf_31s_future_rejected() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 s from now — beyond the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, json) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n// ── ops_matrix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ops_matrix() {\n    use http::Method;\n\n    struct Case {\n        method: Method,\n        ops: Vec<Op>,\n        expected_status: u16,\n    }\n    let cases = vec![\n        // GET (Read) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        // Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: case.ops,\n            }],\n        };\n        let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n        let state = make_state();\n        // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\n                TEST_PATH,\n                axum::routing::any(handler),\n            )\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n                state,\n                jwt_verify_layer,\n            ));\n\n        let req = Request::builder()\n            .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            resp.status().as_u16(),\n            case.expected_status,\n            \"method={} expected {}\",\n            case.method,\n            case.expected_status\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── error_code / status_code consistency ──────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn auth_error_status_and_code_coverage() {\n    // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, \"credential_expired\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid, 401, \"credential_not_yet_valid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialRevoked, 401, \"credential_revoked\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer, 401, \"credential_foreign_issuer\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant, 403, \"credential_wrong_tenant\"),\n        (AuthError::UserSuspended, 401, \"user_suspended\"),\n        (AuthError::NotATenantMember, 403, \"not_a_tenant_member\"),\n        (AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted, 403, \"database_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::OpNotGranted, 403, \"op_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsExceedIssuerRole, 401, \"grants_exceed_issuer_role\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsMalformed, 401, \"grants_malformed\"),\n    ];\n    for (err, expected_status, expected_code) in cases {\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.status_code(),\n            *expected_status,\n            \"status mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.error_code(),\n            *expected_code,\n            \"code mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── revocation via storage fallback ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_via_storage_fallback() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build storage with the JTI revoked at the storage level.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    storage.revoke_jti(jti_uuid);\n\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
